danger_signals.visual_form: Polar bear sprite wearing sunglasses and pink speedo, appears on screen when player delays progression
danger_signals.meaning: Time pressure danger — screen will force upward, crushing player if they cannot keep pace

danger_signals.visual_form: Icicle sprites forming and falling from platform bottoms
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard — instant death threat from above

danger_signals.visual_form: Blue seal (JP) or yeti-like creature (Western), moving horizontally on ice layers
danger_signals.meaning: Mobile threat — can fill floor openings with ice, blocking escape routes and forcing player into hazardous jumping

danger_signals.visual_form: Small mountain bird sprite swooping from cave edges, can cross multiple ice layers
danger_signals.meaning: Unpredictable vertical threat — moves across multiple platforms, harder to predict than confined enemies

danger_signals.visual_form: Ice blocks rendered with cross-hatch visual texture
danger_signals.meaning: Conveyor belt hazard — will slide player left or right uncontrollably

danger_signals.visual_form: Ice blocks with higher detail/shading than standard blocks
danger_signals.meaning: Impassable obstacle — forces alternate route, prevents direct vertical climb

safety_signals.visual_form: Plain, low-detail ice blocks rendered uniformly
safety_signals.meaning: Safe platform — destructible barrier and reliable foothold

safety_signals.visual_form: Cloud-shaped platforms resembling cumulus clouds, moving in predictable patterns
safety_signals.meaning: Safe navigation aid — reliable moving platform for climbing, alternative to ice destruction

safety_signals.visual_form: Level summit area with vegetable/food sprites visible
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone with resource reward — no enemies spawn here, only environmental platforming challenges

safety_signals.visual_form: Corn, eggplant, and other produce sprites at peak locations
safety_signals.meaning: Resource reward — especially eggplant and corn signal extra lives and progression

faction_indicators.color_identity: Popo: blue parka; Nana: pink parka
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Summit peaks, vegetable collection points, bonus stage zones

faction_indicators.color_identity: Blue (seal, JP version) or brown/tan (yeti, Western versions)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Regional variant: seal vs. yeti reflects cultural taboo (clubbing seals controversial in West)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Topi caves on level edges, icicle construction sites, ice layers they defend

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Flies overhead at mountain peak, steals vegetables (establishes climber motivation)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: VS. Ice Climber replaces with giant butterfly in later stage cycles
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears only at peak summit location, marks level completion goal

faction_indicators.color_identity: Small bird sprite (specific color not detailed)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Swooping attack pattern from cave edges
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Icy caves on level edges, vertical flight paths across layers

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Sunglasses and pink speedo (humorous visual identifier)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears off-screen, forces upward screen scroll when player delays

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemies move at standard speed, ice block patterns are simpler, fewer icicle hazards
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Topis move slowly, Nitpickers have predictable sweep patterns, White Bear does not appear

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All enemy sprites display increased animation frames, move faster across screen, more dense icicle spawning
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All enemies accelerate speed, Topi ice-filling becomes more aggressive, Nitpickers swoop more frequently, White Bear begins appearing if player stalls

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen transitions to peak location, all enemies disappear, vegetable items become visible and selectable
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Timer appears (40-second limit), only environmental platforming hazards active, no hostile enemies

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Player time pressure / stalling too long
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: White Bear appears on-screen with visible aggressive pose, screen begins scrolling upward
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forced upward scroll speed increases, player must climb faster or lose life

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ice floor openings are progressively filled with icicles by Topi (visual closure of gaps)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Topi scouts opening, retreats to cave, returns with icicle to block passage; cycle repeats until no openings remain

world_state_to_visual.world_state: VS. Ice Climber campaign progression (8 mountains cleared)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level coloration scheme changes (different color palette), Super Bonus Stage occurs, Condor replaced by giant butterfly sprite
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must reach high platform in Super Bonus, then second set of 48 mountains begins

environmental_storytelling.example: Topi filling ice holes communicates hostile territory defense — Topis own the mountains, Ice Climbers invade to retrieve stolen vegetables
Condor appears only at peak with stolen vegetables — visual confirmation of antagonist and objective

environmental_storytelling.technique: Regional visual substitution for cultural context
environmental_storytelling.example: Seal (JP) vs. Yeti (West) — reflects cultural attitudes; seal-clubbing taboo communicated through sprite swap without narrative change
White Bear with sunglasses/speedo signals humorous-but-deadly time limit; absurd outfit contrasts with genuine danger
Hatched conveyor ice and unbreakable detailed blocks force specific climbing routes — communicates mountain topography and difficulty progression
Peak location with no enemy spawns and visible food items tells player 'this is the goal, now claim the reward'

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves horizontally on single ice layer, confined to that level
Scouts the gap, runs back toward cave entrance
Returns from cave pushing icicle block to fill the gap (repeats until all openings sealed)
Swoops down from cave edge in curved arc, can traverse multiple ice layers, unpredictable entry point
Off-screen, does not appear if player maintains pace
Appears on-screen, audibly pounds ice, forces screen upward at increasing speed
Materialize on platform bottoms after platform has been present for short duration
Flies at peak, player jumps to grab it for bonus score; signals level completion

notes: Ice Climber uses minimal visual language to communicate world-state, relying on sprite swapping (Topi seal/yeti), color/parka distinction (Popo blue/Nana pink), and enemy behavior patterns (Topi scout-retreat-push cycle) to establish faction and danger. No explicit HUD or UI elements communicate story. Regional censorship (seal to yeti swap) demonstrates how visual identity can be altered without narrative change. Time pressure is manifested through the White Bear's absurd appearance (sunglasses/speedo) contrasting with deadly function. Difficulty scaling is communicated purely through enemy speed increase and icicle spawn density, not visual aesthetic shifts. The game's visual grammar is minimal: ice texture variants (dull/detailed/hatched) encode traversal rules; sprite presence indicates threat type; color indicates player identity. Faction conflict is implicit rather than explicit—Topis defend their territory through ice-filling; Condor steals vegetables; climbers ascend to recover them. No political situation complexity exists; conflict is purely goal-driven.
